prompt: NOUN CHOICE for a SECURITY product, 2026. kolm runs an automated, deterministic, self-serve examination of the AI a company ships (permissions, data egress, prompt-injection resistance, audit-trail integrity, etc.) and outputs a SIGNED, independently verifiable report. We want to call the OUTPUT/ACTION the most relatable + accurate noun. Compare: 'security audit', 'security review', 'security assessment', 'security attestation', 'security evidence', 'security report', 'security scan'. Which noun (a) makes a buyer instantly understand + want it, (b) is accurate without overclaiming (e.g. does 'audit' wrongly imply an accredited/CPA/third-party SOC 2 audit? does 'attestation' sound formal/legal?), and (c) is searchable. Cite how 2026 security/devtools products name this (Vanta, pentest-as-a-service like Cobalt/HackerOne, scanners like Snyk/Wiz, attestation/Sigstore, etc., with URLs). Recommend the single best primary noun + when to use the alternates. The founder who shipped the product strongly prefers 'audit' - validate or push back, and if 'audit' is right, how to use it accurately.
content:
**Primary recommendation: "security assessment" (or "AI security assessment").**[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/introducing-vantas-ai-security-assessment)

This is the single best noun for the output/action of kolm based on the criteria. It is instantly relatable to buyers (security/compliance teams routinely discuss and seek "security assessments" or vendor "AI security assessments"), accurate without overclaiming (it describes an evaluation of risks/controls without implying a formal accredited/CPA-driven SOC 2-style audit), and highly searchable/common in the market.

### Why "assessment" wins on the criteria
- **(a) Buyer understanding + desire**: Teams actively look for and reference "security assessments" or "AI security assessments" as actionable evaluations of posture/risks (e.g., Vanta markets its own "AI Security Assessment" as a structured framework for AI risks). It signals value without sounding overly bureaucratic.[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/introducing-vantas-ai-security-assessment)
- **(b) Accuracy without overclaiming**: Distinctions in the industry separate "assessment" (broad risk/vulnerability identification, often automated or self-serve) from formal "audit" (compliance-focused verification, frequently third-party/accredited). Kolm’s automated, deterministic, self-serve nature aligns perfectly with assessment.[[2]](https://linfordco.com/blog/soc-2-vulnerability-assessment-vs-penetration-testing/)[[3]](https://www.dataguard.com/blog/what-is-the-difference-between-cyber-security-audit-and-assessment/)
- **(c) Searchable**: "Security assessment" and "AI security assessment" are widely used and return relevant results across vendor sites, frameworks, and buyer queries.

### Comparison of options
- **Security audit**: Understandable and desirable in compliance circles, but risks overclaiming. Many sources distinguish audits as more formal/compliance-oriented (often involving accredited auditors or SOC 2 attestations) versus lighter automated evaluations. Vanta heavily uses "audit" language for its compliance automation but qualifies it and separately offers "AI Security Assessment."[[4]](https://www.vanta.com/products/audit)[[5]](https://www.vanta.com/collection/grc/compliance-audit)
- **Security review**: Vague/less specific; less commonly the primary marketed noun.
- **Security attestation**: Strong runner-up, especially accurate for kolm’s *signed, independently verifiable* output (directly parallels Sigstore’s use of signed "attestations" for software supply chain claims). It may sound formal/legal to some buyers and is less ubiquitous than "assessment" in general security marketing.[[6]](https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/verifying/attestation/)
- **Security evidence**: Too generic/passive; not a primary action noun.
- **Security report**: Describes the *output* well but is generic and less evocative of the examination process.
- **Security scan**: Accurate for the automated/deterministic part (Snyk/Wiz heavily use "scan"/"scanning" for their tools) but can feel lightweight or limited to vuln detection, underplaying the comprehensive examination and signed report.[[7]](https://www.wiz.io/academy/vulnerability-management/vulnerability-scanning)[[8]](https://docs.snyk.io/scan-fix-and-prevent)

### Market naming examples (2024–2026 products)
- **Vanta**: "Compliance audit software," "audits," and "SOC 2 Type II attestation"/"attestation report" for formal outputs; explicitly "AI Security Assessment" for their AI risk framework.[[4]](https://www.vanta.com/products/audit)[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/introducing-vantas-ai-security-assessment)
- **Pentest-as-a-service (Cobalt, HackerOne)**: "Pentest report," "final PDF report," "assessment’s findings," or "detailed reports."[[9]](https://docs.hackerone.com/en/articles/8541373-pentest-deliverables)[[10]](https://www.hackerone.com/product/pentest)
- **Scanners (Snyk, Wiz)**: Primarily "scan," "scanning," or "vulnerability scanning"; Snyk also references "code security audit" in explanatory content.[[11]](https://snyk.io/articles/code-security-audit/)[[7]](https://www.wiz.io/academy/vulnerability-management/vulnerability-scanning)
- **Attestation (Sigstore and related)**: "Attestations" (e.g., in-toto/artifact attestations) for signed, verifiable claims.[[6]](https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/verifying/attestation/)
- **AI-specific**: "AI Security Assessment" (Vanta, SentinelOne, HITRUST); "risk assessment"; "model scanning."[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/introducing-vantas-ai-security-assessment)[[12]](https://www.sentinelone.com/cybersecurity-101/data-and-ai/ai-security-assessment/)

### Founder preference for "audit" — validation/pushback + usage guidance
Push back on making "audit" the *primary* noun. It is relatable in security circles but carries connotations of formality, accreditation, or third-party CPA involvement (common in SOC 2 contexts), which could set inaccurate expectations for a self-serve automated tool.[[5]](https://www.vanta.com/collection/grc/compliance-audit)[[13]](https://www.fortinet.com/resources/cyberglossary/security-audit) Industry players like Vanta carefully separate their automation from formal "audits."

If the founder strongly prefers it, use "audit" accurately and qualified:
- Primary positioning: "AI security assessment" (or "kolm assessment").
- Alternates/qualified uses: "Automated security audit," "self-serve audit," "kolm audit report," or "continuous/automated audit capabilities" in supporting materials or for compliance-focused buyers. Pair it with "assessment" to manage expectations.
- "Attestation" or "signed attestation" for technical/marketing emphasis on the verifiable signature.

**When to use alternates**:
- **Assessment** (primary): Product name, homepage, buyer-facing descriptions, search/SEO.
- **Attestation**: Technical docs, supply-chain/security proofs, or when highlighting the signed/verifiable report.
- **Audit** (qualified): Compliance/sales conversations or materials targeting audit-heavy teams.
- **Scan/Report**: Developer/technical workflows or when referring specifically to the automated examination step or output document.
- **Evidence**: Internal or when discussing the data backing the output.

This approach keeps messaging accurate, buyer-friendly, and differentiated while respecting the signed/verifiable strength of kolm. "Assessment" (with "attestation" as a close complement) best balances the criteria in the 2026 landscape.
